Regions

Compare Africa by region before narrowing into destination countries and listings.

Each region page leads into destination country landing pages and then into attractions, accommodations, restaurants, and external partner booking links.

Image slot Region East Africa
5 destination countries

East Africa

East Africa remains one of the continent’s highest-demand regions for safari, gorilla trekking, coast add-ons, and premium conservation travel. Kenya and Tanzania lead the classic plains circuits, while Uganda and Rwanda anchor primate-led journeys.

Open region page
Image slot Region West Africa
5 destination countries

West Africa

West Africa is driven by heritage journeys, winter-sun escapes, coastal cities, and community-grounded experiences. Ghana and Senegal are the most polished starting points, while Benin, Sierra Leone, and Cabo Verde broaden the offer considerably.

Open region page
Image slot Region Southern Africa
5 destination countries

Southern Africa

Southern Africa is one of the continent’s most versatile travel regions. South Africa acts as the gateway, Botswana and Namibia deliver high-value wilderness and desert landscapes, and Zimbabwe and Zambia deepen the safari-and-Zambezi story.

Open region page
Image slot Region Northern Africa
4 destination countries

Northern Africa

Northern Africa is Africa’s volume engine for tourism, driven by Morocco, Egypt, Tunisia, and a growing wave of renewed interest in Algeria. Travelers come for cities, heritage, desert camps, Mediterranean coastlines, and food-led travel.

Open region page

Trek Africa Guide

African travel guide and booking directory for regions, destination countries, attractions, stays, dining, and trusted partner booking paths.

Travelers can compare the route logic, nearby stays, dining, and practical access notes on Trek Africa Guide before continuing to external booking partners.
